content.full_text:
.... -
tate of Califorllia 
Fair Political Practices CommissioIl. 
P.o . BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
i·lr. John H. Kuhl 
Cox, Castle, Nicholson 
& Weekes 
Two Century Plaza 
Twenty-Eighth Floor 
2049 Century Park East 
Los Angeles, CA 
90067 
Dear Mr. Kuhl: 
August 25, 1976 
Pursuant to our conversation of August 20, 1976, I 
am writing to clarify the reporting requirements under Chap-
ter 6 of the Political Reform Act for the construction fund. 
Our records reveal that on February 18, 1976 you spoke to 
Nan Hambleton of our staff and were advised that the con-
struction fund had no obligation to file reports pursuant to 
Chapter 6 of the Political Reform Act. 
This advice was 
based on the fact that, at the time, it was unclear what 
filing obligations attended activities of the type engaged 
in by the construction fund since the Commission was in the 
process of deliberating on regulations relevant to the issue. 
In its May, 1976 meeting, the Commission adopted an amend-
ment to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18618 which provides, in 
pertinent part: 
Dues or similar payments made by any person 
for membership in a bona fide association, 
including any federation confederation or 
trade, labor or membership organization, 
some portion of which is used to influence 
legislative or administrative action, need 
not be included for the purpose of deter-
mining whether a person must file reports 
pursuant to Government Code Section 86108(b}. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code §18618(b} 
This amendment made it clear that the filing obli-
gations imposed by Gov. Code Section 86108(b} do not extend 
to entities which pay dues for membership in a bona fide 
association. 
However, the regulation also served to clarify 
that payments which are not within the ambit of the excep-


Mr. John H. Kuhl 
August 25, 1976 
Page Two 
tion established by the amendment can create reporting obli-
gations. 
See Opinion requested by Edwin Sloan, 2 FPPC Ops. 
(No. 75-169, July 6, 1976), a copy of which is attached 
hereto. 
In the case of the construction fund grants, the 
"payments" are not dues or similar payments made for member-
ship in a bona fide association. 
Accordingly, the construc-
tion funds are not covered by the amendment to 2 Cal. Adm. 
Code Section 18618 and, to the extent that their payments 
are payments to influence legislative or administrative 
action within the meaning of Gov. Code Section 82045, the 
construction funds will have reporting obligations under 
Gov. Code Section 86108(b). 
You informed me that all four construction funds 
which we discussed had relied in good faith on the advice 
provided in February, 1976. 
Under these circumstances, all 
reporting requirements imposed upon the construction funds 
will be prospective only, commencing from August 12, 1976. 
If you have any further questions, please do not 
hesitate to call me. 
TP:plh 
Attachment 
cc: 
Paul B. Wells 
Wesley Sizoo 
George M. Cox 
Brian R. Van Camp 
Sincerely,

n

Ted Prim 
Staff Counsel 
Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: What are the reporting requirements under Chapter 6 of the Political Reform Act for construction funds, particularly regarding payments made to influence legislative or administrative action?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Construction fund payments are not dues for membership in a bona fide association and therefore are not covered by the amendment to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18618. To the extent such payments are made to influence legislative or administrative action under Gov. Code Section 82045, construction funds have reporting obligations under Gov. Code Section 86108(b), with requirements being prospective only from August 12, 1976, due to prior good faith reliance on earlier FPPC advice.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections found; extracted question and conclusion from letter body. Letter references prior February 18, 1976 advice that is being clarified/superseded. Good faith reliance provision mentioned for prospective application of requirements.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the reporting requirements under Chapter 6 of the Political Reform Act for construction funds, particularly regarding payments made to influence legislative or administrative action?

CONCLUSION: Construction fund payments are not dues for membership in a bona fide association and therefore are not covered by the amendment to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18618. To the extent such payments are made to influence legislative or administrative action under Gov. Code Section 82045, construction funds have reporting obligations under Gov. Code Section 86108(b), with requirements being prospective only from August 12, 1976, due to prior good faith reliance on earlier FPPC advice.

embedding.summary: The FPPC clarifies reporting obligations for construction funds under the Political Reform Act, clarifying that a May 1976 regulatory amendment exempting membership dues does not apply to construction fund payments, which may trigger reporting obligations if made to influence legislative or administrative action.
